headline: Survey at 22% completion with strong regional concentration; scaling urgency evident.
collection_status: 299 of 1,350 responses collected (22.1% progress). Geographic coverage uneven: Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 56% of responses while Rukwa and Manyara critically underrepresented (9 combined). Respondent base heavily weighted toward teachers (146/299, 49%) with limited district/regional official engagement (14/299, 5%).
key_findings: Expansion is the dominant priority: 110 stakeholders prioritize expanding KiuFunza, with new programs (64) and policy integration (60) following. Research interest minimal (6), suggesting focus on implementation over evaluation.
Partner satisfaction strong (4.4/5) but scale readiness concerning (2.7/5): stakeholders are satisfied but express low confidence in operational capacity to expand effectively.
Feedback consistently emphasizes government partnership, teacher incentives, and digital monitoring as prerequisites for scaling—strategic policy alignment is non-negotiable, not optional.
Severe underrepresentation of district and regional education officers (12 responses vs. expected 25%+ of target): critical stakeholders undersampled, limiting buy-in data for scaling decisions.
flags: Rukwa (5) and Manyara (4) responses critically low; risk survey conclusions skew toward Lindi-Mara-Tanga contexts and mask regional variation.
REOs, DEs, and ARC involvement insufficient (11 responses combined); without strong district/regional voice, scaling recommendations will lack implementation credibility.
Scale readiness (2.7/5) vs. expansion demand mismatch: stakeholders want rapid growth but signal weak operational readiness—risk of overcommitment.
recommendation: Immediately intensify collection in Rukwa, Manyara, and underrepresented western regions via targeted email/phone campaigns to district education officers and DEs. Simultaneously conduct 8-10 rapid phone interviews with DEOs/REOs to bridge the 1,051-response gap and validate scaling assumptions before July milestone decisions.
